llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Selected human_with_five_genai_ideas over human_with_one_genai_idea because it shows larger effect (8.1% vs 5.4% increase in novelty) and represents more capable AI assistance (access to up to 5 ideas vs 1). This is the strongest AI condition tested.
llm_extracted.notes: SALVAGE PASS: Found n_human=95 from table S1 (Human-only condition). The paper reports regression coefficients (b=0.311, p<0.001 for five-ideas condition; b=0.207, p=0.021 for one-idea condition) but does not provide means, SDs, t-statistics, F-statistics, or effect sizes. The only exact p-value available is p=0.021 for the one-idea condition; the five-ideas condition reports only 'p < 0.001' (inequality). Without means+SDs, t-statistics, F-statistics, or reported effect sizes, we can only provide sample sizes and the exact p-value from the weaker comparison. This is Level F extraction (p-value + n only), which yields very low precision for effect size estimation. The p-value of 0.021 is from the one-idea comparison, not the five-ideas comparison we selected as the AI arm, further reducing confidence.
source_quotes: writers in the Human with five GenAI ideas condition show an increase in novelty of 8.1% (b = 0.311, P < 0.001) over writers without generative AI access
writers in the Human with one GenAI idea condition experience an increase of 5.4% (b = 0.207, P = 0.021, see table S3) over writers without generative AI access
table S1: Human-only condition n=95, Human with one GenAI idea n=100, Human with five GenAI ideas n=98
